# **1\. BRIEF PORTFOLIO** {#1.-brief-portfolio}

## **1.1 Professional Experience** {#1.1-professional-experience}

***Associate Professor***	Jul 2021 – Current

***Associate Head (in Learning and Teaching)***	Jul 2021 – Jun 2025

***Assistant Professor***	Aug 2016 – Jun 2021

***Department of Aeronautical and Aviation Engineering, The Hong Kong Polytechnic University, Hong Kong***

* Being the first academic staff in the Department of Aeronautical and Aviation Engineering  
  (co-founding member)

* Manage and revamp the undergraduate degree of aviation engineering as Programme Leader, which is professionally and accredited by Hong Kong Institute of Engineering (HKIE).    
* Fund [Intelligent Positioning and Navigation Laboratory (IPNL)](https://www.polyu.edu.hk/aae/ipn-lab/us/index.html) to advance the science and art in the research of the positioning and navigation for autonomous systems and intelligent devices.

***Visiting Research Scientist*** 		Jan 2023 – Aug 2024

***Android Context team, Google LLC., Mountain View, California, USA***

* Worked with [Dr Frank van Diggelen](https://www.linkedin.com/in/fvandiggelen/) and [Dr Mohammed Khider](https://www.linkedin.com/in/mohammed-khider-b346892a/)  
* Improving GNSS positioning accuracy and reliability for Android devices, including Android phones and Android Auto.  
* Improving absolute Google Map accuracy.

***JSPS Postdoctoral Researcher***	Apr 2014 – Jul 2016

***Institute of Industrial Science, The University of Tokyo, Tokyo, Japan***

* Worked with [Prof Shunsuke Kamijo](https://www.iis.u-tokyo.ac.jp/en/research/staff/shunsuke-kamijo/)  
* Developed lane-level 3D mapping database aided (3DMA) satellite positioning for autonomous driving in deep urban canyons at Tokyo city.  
* Performed experiments for autonomous driving based on multi-sensor integrated system.

***Visiting Researcher*** 	Summer 2013

***Department of Maritime Systems Engineering, Tokyo University of Marine Science and Technology, Tokyo, Japan*** 

* Worked with [Prof Akio Yasuda](https://insidegnss.com/akio-yasuda/) and [Prof Nobuaki Kubo](https://tumsatdb.kaiyodai.ac.jp/html/100000556_en.html)  
* Evaluated an advanced GNSS receiver (vector tracking receiver) in the presence of multipath effect in Tokyo city. 

***Visiting Researcher*** 	Mar 2012 – Feb 2013

***Department of Civil, Environmental, Geomatics Eng., University College London, London, UK*** 

* Worked with [Prof Marek Ziebart](https://profiles.ucl.ac.uk/5255-marek-ziebart) and [Prof Paul Groves](https://profiles.ucl.ac.uk/6850-paul-groves)  
* Developed GNSS vector-tracking receiver, for the anti-interference including multipath and non-light-of-sight (NLOS) effects  
* Developed *Spirent* software MEMS IMU simulators as part-time consultant.

***Part-time Research Assistant*****, NLSC-98-85**	Apr 2009- Mar 2010

***National Land Surveying and Mapping Center, Taichung, Taiwan***

* Worked with [Prof Ming Yang](https://www.geomatics.ncku.edu.tw/english/member.php?id=6&tpl=6), [Prof Kai-Wei Chiang](https://www.geomatics.ncku.edu.tw/english/member.php?id=4&tpl=6) and [Prof Jyh-Ching Juang](https://www.ee.ncku.edu.tw/en/teacher/index2.php?teacher_id=58)  
* Developed wide area differential GNSS both master and reference station systems based on the e-GPS tracking stations. The correction messages are based on the RTCA-DO229 (WAAS MOPS)

***Part-time Research Assistant*****, 97-NSPO(A)-PM-FA09-02** 	Apr 2008- Mar 2009

***National Space Organization, Hsinchu, Taiwan***

* Worked with [Prof Shau-Shiun Jan](https://iaa.ncku.edu.tw/p/412-1104-20365.php?Lang=en)  
* Develop Galileo GIOVE-A signal acquisition, tracking, and multipath mitigation algorithms

## **1.2 Education** {#1.2-education}

**Ph.D. in Aeronautics and Astronautics**	Dec 2013 National Cheng Kung University, Tainan, Taiwan  
Thesis Title: [*Implementation of Vector Tracking Loop in GPS Software Receiver and Its Evaluation in Multipath Environments*](https://hdl.handle.net/11296/eru4us)  
Thesis Supervisor: [Prof Shau-Shiun Jan](https://iaa.ncku.edu.tw/p/412-1104-20365.php?Lang=en)

**B.Sc. in Aeronautics and Astronautics**	Jun 2007 National Cheng Kung University, Tainan, Taiwan

## **1.3 Professional Recognition, Membership and Endowment** {#1.3-professional-recognition,-membership-and-endowment}

[**Limin Young Scholar in Aerospace Navigation**](https://www.polyu.edu.hk/giving/development-priorities/research-and-innovation/endowed-young-scholar-scheme/limin-endowed-young-scholar-in-aerospace-navigation/?sc_lang=en)	2021-2025 

The Limin Endowed Scholar, which is first established in 2021 by the Academic Council with the donation of PolyU Technology and Consultancy Ltd (PTeC). The name of the Scholar is based on PolyU’s motto: *To learn and to apply, for the benefit of mankind*.

**Technical Representative and Council Member**	2019-2021 (1st term)  
		2025-present (2nd term)

**Professional Member** 	2015-present

Institute of Navigation (ION), VA, USA

**Associate Fellow**	2018-present 

Royal Institute of Navigation (RIN), London, UK

**Senior Member**	2021-present 

**Member**	2014-2020 

Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE), USA

* Aerospace and Electronic Systems Society Membership  
* Robotics and Automation Society Membership  
* Intelligent Transportation Systems Society Membership

# **2\. RESEARCH PORTFOLIO** {#2.-research-portfolio}

## **2.1 Research Summary** {#2.1-research-summary}

### ***2.1.1 Research Interests*** {#2.1.1-research-interests}

* Urban navigation,  
  * GNSS signal processing,  
  * GNSS precise positioning,  
  * Multi-sensor fusion,  
  * Indoor positioning and indoor navigation,  
  * Unmanned autonomous systems, and  
  * Intelligent transportation systems.

### ***2.1.2 Summary of Key Research Highlights:*** {#2.1.2-summary-of-key-research-highlights:}

* 145 Journal papers (as of Dec 2025\)  
  * Research bibliometrics (as of Dec 2025\)  
    * [WOS](https://www.webofscience.com/wos/author/record/801467) \- Total citations: 4,841; h-index :39  
    * [Scopus](https://www.scopus.com/authid/detail.uri?authorId=35219760300) \- Total citations: 6,513; h-index :43  
    * [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=GxfOCUMAAAAJ&hl=en) \- Total citations: 8,151; h-index :45; 

## **2.2 Honors: Invited Talks and Lectures in Conferences, Symposiums, Workshops and International Summer School** {#2.2-honors:-invited-talks-and-lectures-in-conferences,-symposiums,-workshops-and-international-summer-school}

1. Invited Organizing Committee Member, Tutorials Chair,

*IEEE Navigation Conference 2026, Munich, Germany, 9–11 Dec 2026\.*

2. Invited Speaker (30 minutes talk), “[Urban GNSS RTK/INS Integrated System: A Perspective on using FGO \+ AI for GNSS](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XOpkI8B89PE&t=308s)” in [IV meets Urban Workshop](https://sites.google.com/view/ivurban2025itsc/2025) at  *IEEE ITSC 2025*, Gold Coast, Australia

3. Invited Panel Speaker (20 minutes talk), “Enabling Robotics Applications with Satellite Navigation: Bridging the Gap with Mathematical Optimization Frameworks,” [ION GNSS+ 2025](https://www.ion.org/gnss/upload/GNSS25-Onsite-2.pdf), Baltimore, Maryland, U.S. 

4. Invited Speaker (30 minutes talk), The Application of Artificial Intelligence in Satellite Navigation: AI-based Urban GNSS Positioning in [ICASSE 2025](https://icasse.sjtu.edu.cn/), Singapore.  

5. Invited Speaker (30 minutes talk), “[Embracing the Power of Non-Gaussian Modeling: A framework for fault detection and integrity monitoring for satellite navigation](https://youtu.be/HdtitiDi-EY?t=3258)” in [IV meets Urban Workshop](https://sites.google.com/view/ivurban2024itsc) at  *IEEE ITSC 2024*, Edmonton, Canada

6. Invited Lecturer (1.5 hours talk), “Indoor Navigation and Indoor Positioning” in Pre-Conference Short Course of [*ION GNSS+ 2024*](https://www.ion.org/gnss/upload/GNSS24-Onsite-Small.pdf) *and [2025](https://www.ion.org/gnss/upload/GNSS25-Onsite-2.pdf)*, invited by ION, USA. 

7. Webinar Speaker (1 hour talk), “[Hong Kong UrbanNav: An Open-Source Multisensory Dataset for Benchmarking Urban Navigation Algorithms](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=V94PAkkpwWs%20)” in ION Webinar, 1 May. 2024, invited by ION, USA. (online mode) 

8. Invited Dinner Speaker (1 hour talk), “Navigating accurately in cities: Vision-sensor-aided GNSS real time kinematic” in [2023 Stanford PNT Symposium, invited by Stanford Center for Positioning, Navigation, Timing of Stanford University](https://scpnt.stanford.edu/about-annual-pnt-symposium/2023-pnt-symposium), USA. 

9. Invited Speaker (1 hour talk), [Safety-aware LiDAR Localization with a Prior Point Cloud Map for Autonomous Driving](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2r2o72nPvrk), in [ION Stanford Section](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/ionavigation_safety-aware-lidar-localization-with-a-prior-activity-7055193472776957952-MIch/), 13 April 2023, invited by ION, USA. 

10. Keynote Speaker (1 hour talk), [Navigation using Graph Optimization: A Case Study of a Smartphone User from an Urban Canyon to an Indoor Area](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=73yvI8ispxY%20), IPIN 2022, 6 Sep 2022, Beijing, China (online mode) 

11. Invited Tutorial Lecturer (3.5 hours lecture), “Indoor Navigation and Positioning” in Pre-Conference Tutorials of [*ION GNSS+ 2020*](https://www.ion.org/gnss/upload/GNSS20Program.pdf)*, [2021](https://www.ion.org/gnss/upload/GNSS21Program.pdf)* and *[2022](https://www.ion.org/gnss/upload/GNSS22Program.pdf)*, invited by ION, USA. (online mode)

12. Webinar Speaker (1 hour talk), “[Factor Graph Optimization for GNSS/INS Integration: A Comparison with the Extended Kalman Filter](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=f5bIh96SRsk)” in ION Webinar, 28 June. 2021, invited by ION, USA. (online mode) 

13. Keynote Speaker (30 minutes talk), “AI in GNSS and collaborative 3D mapping aided GNSS” in *ITSNT 2021*, 24 June. 2021, invited by ENAC, France.  (online mode)

14. Invited Speaker (35 minutes talk), “A Solution for GNSS in Urban Canyon: 3DMA GNSS and Its Development and Future Prospects” in *International Workshop on Autonomous Guidance, Navigation and Control of Unmanned System*, invited by Nanjing University of Aeronautics and Astronautics. Sep., 2020, Nanjing, China. (online mode)

15. Invited Speaker (25 minutes talk), “A New Integrated Navigation Scheme for the Level 4 Autonomous Vehicles in Dense Urban Areas” in *IEEE/ION PLANSx 2020*, invited by Dr. Boris Pervan, Sep., 2020, Missouri, USA (cancelled due to COVID-19).

16. Keynote Speaker (1.5 hour talk), “GNSS Navigation for LBS and Autonomous Driving Applications in Challenging Environments” *in 2019 Huawei Technologies LBS Workshop,* Shanghai, China, 5 Nov. 2019\.

17. Keynote Speaker (25 minutes talk), “Modernized Satellite Navigation for Civil Aviation and Autonomous Vehicle” in *the Fifth International Conference on Aerospace Science & Engineering (ICASE)* invited by *Institute of Space Technology,* Nov., 2017, Islamabad, Pakistan.

18. Invited Lecturer (1 day lecture), “Fundamental of GNSS” in GNSS Summer School 2017, invited by Prof Akio Yasuda, *Institute of Positioning, Navigation and Timing of Japan*, Jul., 2017, Tokyo, Japan.

## **2.3 Research Publications**  {#2.3-research-publications}

### ***2.3.1 Journal Papers*** {#2.3.1-journal-papers}

**2025:**

1. Lee, M. J. L., Lin, J., Zhang, G., & **Hsu, L. T.** (2025). Framework for Low-Cost Indoor Smartphone Camera-based Localization Using Spherical Panorama and BIM. *IEEE Sensors Journal*.  
2. Liu, X., Wen, W., Zhang, L., & **Hsu, L. T.** (2025). 3D LiDAR Aided GNSS NLOS Correction by Direction-of-Arrival Estimation Using Doppler Measurements in Urban Canyons. *IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems*.  
3. Yang, P., Wen, W., Bai, S., & **Hsu, L. T.** (2025). Tightly joined positioning and control model for unmanned aerial vehicles based on factor graph optimization. *IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology*.  
4. Lee, M. J. L., Ng, H. F., & **Hsu, L. T.** (2025). Automated Camera Positioning for Digital Twinning: Incorporating 360-Degree Imaging, Differential Rendering, and 3D Spatial Data. *IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement*.  
5. Zheng, X., Wen, W., & **Hsu, L. T.** (2025). Safety-quantifiable line feature-based monocular visual localization with 3d prior map. *IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems*.  
6. Ng, H. F., Zhong, Q., Groves, P., & **Hsu, L. T.** (2025). Grid-based 3DMA GNSS with clustering and Doppler velocity using factor graph optimisation. *The Journal of Navigation*, 1-19.  
7. Yan, P., Li, Z., Huang, F., Wen, W., & **Hsu, L. T.** (2025). Fault Detection Algorithm for Gaussian Mixture Noises: An Application in Lidar/IMU Integrated Localization Systems. *NAVIGATION: Journal of the Institute of Navigation*, *72*(1).  
8. Bai, S., Wen, W., Su, D., & **Hsu, L. T.** (2025). Graph-Based Indoor 3D Pedestrian Location Tracking With Inertial-Only Perception. *IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing*.  
9. Yan, P., Hu, Y., Wen, W., & **Hsu, L. T. (**2025). Multiple Faults Isolation For Multi-Constellation GNSS Positioning through Incremental Expansion of Consistent Measurements. *IEEE Sensors Journal*.

**2024:**

10. Zhang, L., Ng, H. F., Zhong, Y., Zhang, G., & **Hsu, L. T.** (2024). GNSS Doppler Velocity Estimation Aided by 3D Mapping Database. *IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems*.  
11. Ho, H. Y., Ng, H. F., Wen, W., Gu, Y., & **Hsu, L. T.** (2024). Enhancing Smartphone-based Pedestrian Positioning: Using Factor Graph Optimization with Indoor/Outdoor Detection for 3DMA GNSS/Visual-Inertial State Estimation. *Journal of Data Science and Intelligent Systems*.  
12. Fang, J., Zhang, L., Zhang, G., Xu, B., & **Hsu, L. T.** (2024). Characterization and Validation of GNSS Multipath-Induced Doppler Measurement Error. *IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems*.  
13. Bai, S., Wen, W., Li, Y., Shi, C., & **Hsu, L. T.** (2024). Towards Persistent Spatial Awareness: A Review of Pedestrian Dead Reckoning-Centric Indoor Positioning with Smartphones. *IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement*.

135. Gu Y., **Hsu L. T.**, Bao J., Kamijo S. (2016) Integrating Global Navigation Satellite System and Road-Marking Detection for Vehicle Localization in Urban Traffic, *Transportation Research Record Journal,* 2595(1):59-67.

**2015:**

136. **Hsu L. T.\***, Gu Y., Kamijo S., (2015) NLOS Correction/Exclusion for GNSS Measurement using RAIM and City Building Models, *Sensors*, 15(7):17329-17349.  
137. Gu Y., **Hsu L. T**, Kamijo S., (2015) Passive Sensor Integration for Vehicle Self-Localization in Urban Traffic Environment, *Sensors*, 15(12):30199-30220.   
138. Miura S., **Hsu L. T.**, Chen F., Kamijo S. (2015) GPS Error Correction with Pseudorange Evaluation Using 3-dimensional Maps, *IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems,* 16(6):3104-3115.   
139. **Hsu L. T.\***, Chen F., Kamijo S. (2015) Evaluation of Multi-GNSSs and GPS with 3D Map Methods for Pedestrian Positioning in an Urban Canyon Environment, *IEICE TRANSACTIONS on Fundamentals of Electronics, Communications and Computer Sciences,* E98.A(1):284-293. 

**Before 2015:**

140. **Hsu L. T.**\*, Jan, S. S., Groves, P. D., Kubo N., (2014) Multipath mitigation and NLOS detection using vector tracking in urban”, *GPS Solutions*, 19(2):249-262.   
141. **Hsu L. T.\***, Jan, S. S. (2013) Performance Analysis of Advanced GPS Receiver Technique in Radio Frequency Difficult Environment, *Journal of Aeronautics, Astronautics and Aviation*, *Series A*, 45(3):203-210.

### ***2.3.3 Industrial Orientated Magazines Articles*** {#2.3.3-industrial-orientated-magazines-articles}

1. **Hsu L. T.**, “[What are the roles of artificial intelligence and machine learning in GNSS positioning](https://insidegnss.com/what-are-the-roles-of-artificial-intelligence-and-machine-learning-in-gnss-positioning/)?” *Inside GNSS*, November-December 2020\.  
2. Wen W, **Hsu** **L. T.\***, " [Perceived Environment Aided GNSS Single Point Positioning: An Example using LiDAR Scanner](https://insidegnss.com/perceived-environment-aided-gnss-single-point-positioning-an-example-using-lidar-scanner/)," *Inside GNSS*, July-August 2019\.   
3. Kamijo S., Gu Y., **Hsu** **L. T.**, "[Autonomous Vehicle Technologies: Localization and Mapping](https://www.jstage.jst.go.jp/article/essfr/9/2/9_131/_article/-char/en)", *IEICE Fundamentals Review*, 9(2):131-141, October 2015\.  
4. **Hsu L. T.\***, Miura S., Kamijo S., “[Street Smart: 3-D City Mapping and Modeling for Positioning with Multi-GNSS](https://www.gpsworld.com/street-smart-3d-city-mapping-and-modeling-for-positioning-with-multi-gnss/),” *GPS World*, 26(7):36-43, July 2015\.  
5. **Hsu L. T.\***, Tsai W. M., Jan S. S., “[Real Time Indoor Location Based Service Test Bed (II)](https://mycoordinates.org/pdf/may11.pdf),” *Coordinates Magazine*, 7(5):32-36, May 2011\.  
6. **Hsu L. T.**\*, Tsai W. M., Jan S. S., “Real Time Indoor Location Based Service Test Bed (I),” *Coordinates Magazine*, Volume 7, Issue 4, pp. 27-29, April 2011\.

3. **Hsu L. T.\***, Wen W. and Zhang G, “Chapter 2 \- Vehicular Positioning System” of the book “[Vehicular Networks: Applications, Performance Analysis and Challenges](https://novapublishers.com/shop/vehicular-networks-applications-performance-analysis-and-challenges/),” *Nova Science Publishers Inc*., September 2019\.  
   

### ***2.3.3 Open-Source Codes and Data*** {#2.3.3-open-source-codes-and-data}

* Xu B. **Hsu L. T.\*** (2019) **An Open Source Matlab Code on GPS Vector Tracking based on Software-Defined Receiver**, *GPS Solutions,*23(2):46. The Matlab Code can be download via [https://www.ngs.noaa.gov/gps-toolbox/GPS\_VT\_SDR.htm](https://www.ngs.noaa.gov/gps-toolbox/GPS_VT_SDR.htm)

* Wen W., **Hsu, L.T.\*** (2021) **Towards Robust GNSS Positioning and Real-time Kinematic Using Factor Graph Optimization**, *IEEE ICRA 2021\.* The C/C++ Code can be download via: [https://github.com/weisongwen/GraphGNSSLib](https://github.com/weisongwen/GraphGNSSLib)   
    
* **Hsu, L. T.\***, Huang, F., Ng, H. F., Zhang, G., Zhong, Y., Bai, X., & Wen, W. (2023). **Hong Kong UrbanNav: An open-source multisensory dataset for benchmarking urban navigation algorithms**. *NAVIGATION: Journal of the Institute of Navigation*, *70*(4). 

[https://github.com/IPNL-POLYU/UrbanNavDataset](https://github.com/IPNL-POLYU/UrbanNavDataset)

2. Ng, Hoi-Fung, Multi-Epoch Kriging-Based 3D Mapping Aided GNSS Using Factor Graph Optimization, *ION GNSS+, 2022,* Denver, Colorado, USA. (**Best Student Paper Award**)

3. Zhang, G., 3D Mapping Aided GNSS-Based Cooperative Positioning Using Factor Graph Optimization, *ION GNSS+, 2019,* Miami, Florida, USA. (**Best Student Paper Award**)

4. Zhang, G., **Hsu, Li-Ta**, December 10-13, 2017, " Adaptive GNSS/INS Integration Based on Supervised Machine Learning Approach," *Proceedings of International Symposium on GNSS*, Hong Kong. (**Best Student Paper Award**)

**Best Presentation Awards**

1. Xu, Penghui, Ng, Hoi-Fung, Zhong Yihan, Zhang, Guohao Wen, Weisong, Yang, Bo, **Hsu L. T.**, Differentiable Factor Graph Optimization with Intelligent Covariance Adaptation for Accurate Smartphone Positioning, *ION GNSS+, 2023,* Denver, Colorado, USA. (**Best Presentation Award**)

### ***3.4.2 Students’ response (aspects of this staff member's teaching were most helpful to your learning) are abstracted:***  {#3.4.2-students’-response-(aspects-of-this-staff-member's-teaching-were-most-helpful-to-your-learning)-are-abstracted:}

•	He is always passionate in his lectures, making the lectures always interesting to attend

•	Dr Hsu presented the teaching material in a systematic way and very clear to make me easy to understand.

•	The example is very vivid, easy to understand, the teacher is very humorous, very like.

•	The teacher is really nice and I've never met a teacher like this. He teaches us in a funny way. When we come across that we don't know, he always welcomes student to ask. I'm very glad that he teaches us throughout this semester, most of our students really love him :)

•	The extra information and experience was interesting and useful. It is easy to understand what avionics system is.

•	Good speaking help me to understand the content easily

•	Best Lecturer I ever meet.

•	Every lecture given by Mr. Hsu was amazing. His presentation skill is great that helping us easily understand the materials. He even well organized the makeup classes, quickly replied every email when he was in hospital as he got injury in the accident. I really look forward to every of his lecture.

•	Nice and interesting teaching, always elaborate some difficult concepts in a simple way that helps students to learn better

•	Lecturer used clear PowerPoint and spoke clearly (even if at a rapid pace).

## **3.5 Development of Curriculum and Pedagogy (Subject Level)**  {#3.5-development-of-curriculum-and-pedagogy-(subject-level)}

### ***3.5.1 New Freshman Seminar Project – ENG1003 – Hovercraft Design and Manufacture*** {#3.5.1-new-freshman-seminar-project-–-eng1003-–-hovercraft-design-and-manufacture}

The department starts to offer an aircraft related project in the subject of ENG1003 Freshman Seminar for Engineering. I developed a project to design and manufacture the hovercraft from the scratch. I chose hovercrafts because its dynamics is similar to aircraft that using aerodynamics. Every year, our first-year students will have a hovercraft competition using the hovercraft they built (see below). The students’ feedback indicate this project is very popular of among the freshman projects. The project is being adopted to some secondary schools in Hong Kong

![A group of people posing for a photoDescription automatically generated][image6]

Fig. Hovercraft competition of ENG1003 of 2018 cohort of students

### ***3.5.2 New Freshman Seminar Project – ENG1003 – Design of Path Planning Algorithm for Aircraft Operation (Online Project for Virtual Classroom due to COVID-19)*** {#3.5.2-new-freshman-seminar-project-–-eng1003-–-design-of-path-planning-algorithm-for-aircraft-operation-(online-project-for-virtual-classroom-due-to-covid-19)}

Due to the COVID-19, a new online group project is required since students are coming different countries and regions. The project is expected to equipment with the abilities on 1\) leaning to learn 2\) collaboration and 3\) problem. We design a project of aircraft path planning based A\* algorithm using an open-source code in *Python Robotics*. We will also teach students the concept and the use of *GitHub* so that the students can collaborate online to write the codes and reports together. Students are expected to design an A\* path planning algorithm for different models (including A320, A330, etc) of Airbus aircraft to achieve the shortest route and minimal cost. 

### ***3.5.3 Granted the PolyU Internal Fund on Large Equipment for Teaching Purpose***  {#3.5.3-granted-the-polyu-internal-fund-on-large-equipment-for-teaching-purpose}

My role is the Leader of this project team. We wish to develop students’ understanding and capability in flight mechanics and guidance, navigation and control (GNS). We secured HK$1,132,815( USD$145,000) to purchase:  
•	Aircraft Satellite Navigation System (NovAtel SPANCPT) – HK$ 237,400   
•	Light Detection and Ranging Radar (Velodyne LiDAR) – HK$ 303,915  
•	Vison-based Motion Capture System (VICON) – HK$ 591,500   
These equipment provide students to see, touch, play and learn the flight mechanics, guidance and control systems of an aircraft and further train students with concrete knowledge of autonomous vehicle control and navigation systems. Several groups of undergraduate students used these equipment for their final year project (FYP). Figures below are examples the students used LiDAR and VICON for indoor mobile robot under ROS and the robot they used. 

![A computer screen shot of a computerDescription automatically generated][image7]![A machine with wires and a plastic coverDescription automatically generated][image8]  
Fig. (left) LiDAR Point Visualization (right) Kuka robot with LiDAR sensor.

### ***3.5.4 Development of Laboratory Activities in Avionics***  {#3.5.4-development-of-laboratory-activities-in-avionics}

In my capacity as Subject Leader of AAE4301, I developed two avionics related activities to enhance students’ learning experience. 

* Flight Simulator

Flight simulators with the model of Cessna 172 Skyhawk are used for students’ practice. The aim of the lab is to learn the importance of avionics, including air data computer, inertial sensors, VOR, DME and GPS, in pilot flight operations. Photos extracted from the students’ report are shown below. 

![A control panel with buttons and switchesDescription automatically generated][image9]![A screen with a calendarDescription automatically generated][image10] **![A map of the oceanDescription automatically generated][image11]**  
Fig (left) student operating flight simulator (middle) navigation plan based on VOR/DME (right) Student’s flight route from HK airport to Taiwan airport.

The students’ responses on the lab activities are abstracted below:

* The flight simulator is a useful interface to understand the flight operation and the function and operation of the flight instrument. It is very useful for learning rather than read the book.  
* The flight simulation lab is a great activity to help us learn the knowledge by heart.  
* Learning on flight simulator to get knowledge and do application of navigation

* Avionics Maintenance

The aircraft avionics equipment installed in the trainer is actual aircraft avionics equipment and this equipment is wired using actual aircraft wire and electrical components such as power relays and circuit breakers. The aim of this lab to simulate a testing environment to train our students in avionic maintenance. 

![Close-up of a device with a screen and a displayDescription automatically generated][image12]  
Fig Student operating avionics maintenance instrumentation 

## **3.6 Management of Subjects and Programmes:**  {#3.6-management-of-subjects-and-programmes:}

### ***3.6.1 Service as Program Leader and other Programme related matters*** {#3.6.1-service-as-program-leader-and-other-programme-related-matters}

I have been serving in the DUPC/DLTC since 2016 and provide input on programme related matters on a regular basis. Since May 2018, I am serving as the Programme Leader for the Bachelor of Engineering (Honours) in Aviation Engineering (AE). The DSE entry score of the AE programme ranked second in faculty of engineering (FENG) (only behind the biomechanical programme) in 2019/20 cohort. I am responsible for admission, credit transfer, career placement, programme promotion and other related matters. I am also the committee member of the programme of Bachelor of Engineering (Hons) in Air Transport Engineering (two-year top-up degree) (ATE). During my capacity as programme leader, we initiate the programme revamp aiming to the undergraduate students’ professional training development.

### ***3.6.2 Quality Assurance Activities for HKIE Provisional and Full Accreditation of AE Programme in 2018 and 2020, respectively.***  {#3.6.2-quality-assurance-activities-for-hkie-provisional-and-full-accreditation-of-ae-programme-in-2018-and-2020,-respectively.}

In my capacity as Programme Leader of AE and DLTC member (later chairman), I was involved in coordinating, reviewing submission documents, and presenting together with Prof CW Leung and HoD for HKIE accreditation exercise in 2018\. The AE programme starts the first cohort in 2016 and conducts its learning outcomes assessment plan (LOAP) ever since. Therefore, the 2018 exercise is the first accreditation of the programme. The outcome is 5-years provisional accreditation in the stream of aircraft (ACE), logistics & transportation (LTE), and manufacturing & industrial (MIE) engineering. The programme will be reviewed again until the programme has the first cohort of graduates. 

In 2020, I am responsible for leading the team to prepare the submission documents and presenting to HKIE visit team together with HoD for HKIE full accreditation exercise in November 2020\. The AE programme has the first batch of graduate in 2022 and conducts its new learning outcomes assessment plan (LOAP) with **the newly developed subject-based rubrics**. Meanwhile, I propose forming a focus group on the quality assurance and monitoring in the DAC meeting to make sure the achievements of the programme learning outcome (PLOs) of AE programme. The 2020 exercise is the second accreditation of the programme. The outcome will be released in 2021\. 

## **3.7 Promotion of Teaching Quality among Peers.** {#3.7-promotion-of-teaching-quality-among-peers.}

### ***3.7.1 Developed Subject based Rubrics for AE Programme and Revamped the Subject Learning Outcomes for AAE Core Subjects*** {#3.7.1-developed-subject-based-rubrics-for-ae-programme-and-revamped-the-subject-learning-outcomes-for-aae-core-subjects}

In my capacity as Programme Leader of AE and DLTC chairman, I am responsible to develop subject-based rubrics (together with the efforts of Dr Frank Zou and Dr Kam Ng) to better assess the achievements of subject intended learning outcomes (SLOs). The subject-based rubrics are also used to indicate the 5 ability levels (Excellent, Good, Satisfactory, Marginal and Unsatisfactory) that a student needs to attain, in accordance to the SLOs that defined in in the subject description forms (SDF) in order to receive a certain grade. Meanwhile, the relationships between the assessed SLOs and PLOs are also defined in the SDF This newly developed assessment and monitoring plan is visualized in the Figure below. Red parts denote the new developments. During the development, the SDF of the AAE core subjects, that used to measure PLOs, are revamped. 

![][image13]

Fig. The flowchart of the proposed assessment of PLOs of AE based on the newly developed subject based rubrics. 

### ***3.7.2 Advised a Colleague to Apply an Internal Teaching Grant of the PolyU***  {#3.7.2-advised-a-colleague-to-apply-an-internal-teaching-grant-of-the-polyu}

I advised Dr Peng LU on the preparation of the proposal for our application to purchase Large equipment for teaching purpose. I was actively advised Dr Lu on the preparation and procedure but also am engaged as team member to contribute to this teaching project. The grant is successfully secured HK$1,020,915 to procure autonomous system for FYP projects. 

### ***3.7.3 Role of Reviewer for Peer Review of Teaching Practice*** {#3.7.3-role-of-reviewer-for-peer-review-of-teaching-practice}

I joined the workshop held by EDC PolyU and became eligible for Peer Review of Teaching Practice. In my roles as a peer reviewer for teaching practice, I assessed Dr Chee Tong, who is looking for promotion.  The review went smooth and Dr Tong promoted from a Teaching Fellow to a Senior Teaching Fellow. 

# **4\. SERVICE PORTFOLIO (ACADEMIC)** {#4.-service-portfolio-(academic)}

## **4.1 Elected Technical Representative**  {#4.1-elected-technical-representative}

**In January 2019, Dr Li-Ta Hsu is elected as the one of the 8 technical representatives in ION.** The Institute of Navigation (ION) is a not-for-profit professional organization dedicated to advancing Positioning, Navigation and Timing (PNT). ION’s international membership is drawn from many sources including professional navigators, engineers, physicists, mathematicians, astronomers, cartographers, photogrametrists, meteorologists, educators, geodesists, surveyors, **general aviation and airline pilots, mariners and anyone interested in position-determining systems**. Corporate members include corporations, civil and military government agencies, private scientific and technical institutions, universities and training academies, and consulting firms. **In January 2025, Dr Li-Ta Hsu is elected again as the one of the 8 technical representatives in ION.**

![][image14]![][image15]

![][image16]  
Figure: (Up-left) Election result of ION officer for 2019-2021. (Up-right) Function of a technical representative. (Bottom) Columnist interview Li-Ta’s vision in the development and challenge of navigation, full story at: [https://www.ion.org/newsletter/upload/v29n4.pdf](https://www.ion.org/newsletter/upload/v29n4.pdf).

| Using Machine Learning and 3D Maps to Solve GNSS Multipath/NLOS Effects for Autonomous Driving Vehicle | *Beijing Research Institute of Telemetry of China Aerospace Science and Technology Corporation* | Beijing, China, 26 Jan. 2018 |

## **4.4 Chair, Working Group on Positioning and Navigation at Asian Urban Canyons under ION (Institute of Navigation)/IAG (International Association of Geodesy)** {#4.4-chair,-working-group-on-positioning-and-navigation-at-asian-urban-canyons-under-ion-(institute-of-navigation)/iag-(international-association-of-geodesy)}

Chair: Dr Li-Ta Hsu (AAE, PolyU)  
Co-Chair: Prof Nobuaki Kubo (Tokyo University of Marine Science \&Technology)  
Members:

* Dr Taro Suzuki (Chiba Institute of Technology)  
* Prof Junichi Meguro (Meijo University)  
* Prof Wu Chen (LSGI, PolyU)  
* Prof Zhizhao Liu (LSGI, PolyU)

The objectives are:

* Open-sourcing localization sensor data, including GNSS, INS, LiDAR and cameras collected in Asian urban canyons, including Tokyo and Hong Kong.  
  * Raising the awareness of the urgent navigation requirement in highly-urbanized areas, especially in Asian-Pacific regions.  
  * Providing an integrated online platform for data sharing to facilitate the development of navigation solutions of the research community.  
  * Benchmarking positioning algorithms based on open-sourcing data.

  *Progress Reported in ION GNSS+ 2019 and ION ITM 2020\. And the journal paper is published in [NAVIGATION](https://navi.ion.org/content/navi/70/4/navi.602.full.pdf).* 

  The detail of the open-source data can be found at [https://github.com/IPNL-POLYU/UrbanNavDataset](https://github.com/IPNL-POLYU/UrbanNavDataset) 

![A screenshot of a computerDescription automatically generated][image19] ![A machine with cameras on itDescription automatically generated][image20]  
Fig. (Left) Webpage of the open-source data website. (Right) The equipment set we used to collect the data, including LiDAR, monocular camera, IMU, GNSS receivers (both geodetic and commercial level).
